km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: William De Bella Aqua出生在英國。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William De Bella Aqua出生在英国。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Romeo, Salvatore, Aldo, Alfonso, Alfredo, Antonio, Berto, Carlo, Dario, Italia, Napoli, Sebastiano, Vita.  Spanish Miguel, Carmelita, Eleuterio, Elida, Graciela, Juan, Lazaro, Maria De La Luz.
Italian and Spanish: from the female personal name Bella, meaning ‘beautiful, lovely’ (see Bello ). Compare Di Bella .
Italian: possibly also a habitational name from Basilicata Bella, in Potenza, probably so named with Latin labellum ‘little basin’.

variants.name: Bellina
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Emanuele, Pio, Salvatore.
Italian: from a diminutive of the female personal name   Bella  .
Americanized or Germanized form of Polish, Slovenian or other Slavic   Belina  . This is also an Italianized form of the Slovenian name.

variants.name: Bellas
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a belltower, from Middle English bel-hous ‘detached bell-house or belfry’ (Old English bell-hūs). The surname is now found chiefly in Yorkshire and Westmorland.
Greek: Hellenized form of the Italian surname Bella , or alternatively a nickname derived from South Slavic bel ‘white’.
Galician: probably from the adjective belo, bela ‘beautiful, lovely’, possibly with topographic reference.

variants.name: Di Bella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Salvatore, Carmela, Cosimo, Rosario, Angelo, Alberto, Antonio, Agostino, Biagio, Carmelo, Carmine, Dino, Emilio, Fernando, Francesco, Leonardo, Renato, Ricardo, Roberto.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name   Bella  . Compare   De Bella  .

variants.name: Bellia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Augustino, Carmela, Sal, Salvatore, Agatino, Attilio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaetano.
Italian (Sicily) and Maltese: variant of   Bella  ‘beautiful’.

variants.name: Bello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Jorge, Mario, Roberto, Eduardo, Sergio, Alberto, Ernesto, Julio.  Portuguese Joaquim, Paulo.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Natale, Vito, Eliseo, Luciano, Sal, Aldo, Amato.

Spanish and Italian: from bello ‘handsome’ (from Late Latin bellus), hence a nickname for a handsome man or perhaps in some instances ironically for an ugly one. In medieval Italy the word was also applied as an omen or well-wishing name.
Catalan (Belló): shortened form of Abelló (see Abello ).

variants.name: De Bella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvato, Salvatore, Santo.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name   Bella  . Compare   Di Bella  .

nameMeanings.name: Aqua
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: status name from Old French laquais, a term denoting (in the 16th century) an auxiliary soldier, e.g. army courier.

variants.name: Laque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Arturo, Jaime, Leticia, Ruben.
Catalan (Laqué): variant of Llaqué, itself possibly a variant of Llaquet, a topographic name from a diminutive of llac ‘pond’. This surname is very rare in Spain.
German (also Laqué): variant of Laqua . This surname is rare in Germany.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

es.lifeSummary: William De Bella Aqua nació en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
